codeReview.summary: The DaemonError enum is fully implemented with all required variants (TaskNotFound, ValidationFailed with ValidationReason sub-enum, HookDenied, BudgetExceeded with BudgetClass sub-enum, WorktreeAllocFailed, CommitFailed, Llm, Config, ShuttingDown, Internal). The implementation correctly derives thiserror::Error, includes JSON serialization via DaemonErrorWire with serde tag, and thiserror appears in swell-daemon/Cargo.toml.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the project convention of using local error types (WorktreeError, GitError, ConfigError, LlmError) defined within swell-daemon rather than importing from other crates. This is consistent with the pattern seen in swell-core's SwellError which also defines local variants. The feature description did not specify whether to import or define locally, and the worker made a reasonable choice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-daemon/src/error.rs: WorktreeError, GitError, ConfigError, LlmError are all defined locally in error.rs

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust skill was used for this feature. The worker followed proper procedure by reading mission.md, validation-contract.md, Cargo.toml, and existing error types before implementing. No deviations from the skill's procedure were noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: Read mission.md, validation-contract.md, Cargo.toml, error.rs, followed by Grep for WorktreeError/GitError/LlmError patterns, Skill rust invocation

summary: Feature refactor-04-define-error-enum passes review. DaemonError enum with all 10 required variants, ValidationReason (5 variants), BudgetClass (3 variants), and local error types (WorktreeError, GitError, ConfigError, LlmError) are correctly implemented. thiserror::Error derive is applied correctly. DaemonErrorWire provides JSON serialization. From conversions for SwellError and anyhow::Error are implemented. All verification steps pass (cargo check exits 0, thiserror in Cargo.toml, 84 tests pass).
